seoTitle: Great Sandy Strait vs Green Island — Climate Risk Comparison | ClimateNest
seoDescription: Compare climate risk scores between Great Sandy Strait and Green Island. See which suburb is safer from flood, bushfire, heat, and storm hazards.
ogTitle: Great Sandy Strait vs Green Island: Climate Safety
ogDescription: Data-driven comparison of climate risk in Great Sandy Strait and Green Island.
heading: Great Sandy Strait vs Green Island — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between Great Sandy Strait, Queensland and Green Island, Queensland. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: Great Sandy Strait has a lower overall climate risk score (33/100) compared to Green Island (36/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is Great Sandy Strait or Green Island safer from flooding?
faq.answer: Great Sandy Strait has a flood risk score of 26/100, while Green Island scores 56/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: Great Sandy Strait scores 21/100 for bushfire risk. Green Island scores 41/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in Great Sandy Strait or Green Island?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. Great Sandy Strait has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.

valueImpact.description: Properties in this area may face a 0.88% climate value drag over time due to rising insurance costs and climate adaptation expenses.
